event: Playing in a “lazy river” at a water amusement park
Became trapped against a large suction intake drain
Brought to the emergency department after a near-drowning incident
Diffuse abrasions on his torso in the configuration of a square
Abdomen was without any peritoneal signs
Air extending to the neck on chest x-ray
CT scan revealed intraabdominal free fluid particularly in the region of the esophageal hiatus
Laparotomy revealed gross contamination with food debris
No surgical proximal esophageal diversion performed
Large bore nasogastric tube placed in the esophagus above the staple line
Transferred to Pediatric Intensive Care Unit
Parenteral nutrition was started in immediate postoperative period
Fluoroscopic evaluation demonstrated a leak in the esophagus
Leak in esophagus was controlled by mediastinal drain
Enteral nutrition was initiated via gastrostomy tube
Discharged on day 50 with nasogastric tube and mediastinal drain
Underwent Ivor-Lewis distal esophagectomy with gastric pull-up for esophagogastric anastomosis
Midline abdominal incision was made using previous incision site
Gastric conduit was made along the lesser curvature using surgical GIA stapler
Tubularized portion of the stomach advanced into chest
29 mm end-to-end anastomosis stapler used to complete esophagogastric anastomosis
Leak test performed via upper endoscopy was negative
Nutritionally supported with jejunostomy enteral feeds
On day 7, esophagram showed no leak at anastomosis
Can tolerate a regular diet without difficulty
